title: Mangla Gauri Temple
tagline: Mangla Gauri Temple — spiritual town of Bihar

overview.short: Mangla Gauri Temple (Hindi: मां मंगलागौरी मंदिर) is a Hindu temple and Shakta pitha, in Gaya, Bihar, India was mentioned in Padma Purana, Vayu Purana and Agni Purana and Devi Bhagv
overview.description: Mangla Gauri Temple (Hindi: मां मंगलागौरी मंदिर) is a Hindu temple and Shakta pitha, in Gaya, Bihar, India was mentioned in Padma Purana, Vayu Purana and Agni Purana and Devi Bhagvata Purana and Markandeya Purana in other scriptures and tantric works. This temple is among the eighteen maha Shakta pithas. The present temple dates back to the 15th century. The shrine is dedicated to Sati or the Hindu Mother Goddess in the predominantly Vaishnavite pilgrimage center of Gaya.

topPlaces.name: Government Polytechnic, Gaya
topPlaces.description: Government Polytechnic, Gaya is a polytechnic institute in Bihar, India. It is administered by Department of Science and Technology, Bihar and is funded by Government of Bihar.

topPlaces.name: White House Compound (Gaya)
topPlaces.description: White House Compound (Hindi: व्हाइट हाउस कंपाउंड, Urdu: وائٹ ہاؤس کمپاؤنڈ) is a residential locality in Judges Colony under the Rampur thana jurisdiction of Gaya, Bihar, India. It serves the Mirza Ghalib College and Smart Bazaar earlier Big Bazaar Mall.

topPlaces.name: Durga Bari (Gaya)
topPlaces.description: Durga Bari (Hindi: दुर्गाबाड़ी, romanized: durgā-bāṛī) is a residential-cum commercial neighborhood under the Civil Lines thana jurisdiction of Gaya, Bihar, India. It is located south of Dulhingunj neighborhood while facing Falgu River eastwards.

topPlaces.name: Nazareth Academy, Gaya
topPlaces.description: Nazareth Academy (formerly St. Michael’s School, 1939–1953) is a private Roman Catholic secondary school in Old Karimganj, Gaya, Bihar, India.

topPlaces.name: Chatta Masjid
topPlaces.description: Chatta Masjid (Hindi: छत्ता मस्जिद, IAST: Chattā Masjida; Urdu: چھتہ مسجد, romanized: Chatta Masjid), also spelled as Chhatta Masjid, is a congregational mosque located in Dulhingunj neighborhood of Gaya, Bihar, India, close to Falgu river. Popular for being situated at a bustling marketplace, the mosque also serves as a landmark for nearby bakeries that sell traditional bakarkhanis and sheermals besides wholesale shops that import dates from the Middle East.

topPlaces.name: Old Karimganj (Gaya)
topPlaces.description: Old Karimganj (Hindi: पुरानी करीमगंज, IAST: Purānī Karīmagaṃja; Urdu: پرانی کریم گنج, romanized: Purānī Karīmganj), also spelled as Old Kareemganj or simply Karimganj, is a residential neighborhood under the Civil Lines thana zone of Gaya, Bihar, India, inhabited mostly by Muslims. Forming the eastern wing of the larger Karimganj neighborhood, it shares its proximity with New Karimganj neighborhood to the west and Jagdeo Nagar to the north.

topPlaces.name: New Karimganj (Gaya)
topPlaces.description: New Karimganj (Hindi: न्यू करीमगंज, IAST: Nyū Karīmagaṃja; Urdu: نیو کریم گنج, romanized: Niū Karīmganj), also spelled as New Kareemganj or simply Karimganj, is a residential neighborhood under the Civil Lines thana zone of Gaya, Bihar, India, inhabited mostly by Muslims. Forming the western wing of the larger Karimganj neighborhood, it shares its proximity with Old Karimganj neighborhood to the east and Jagdeo Nagar to the north.

topPlaces.name: Maroofganj (Gaya)
topPlaces.description: Maroofganj (Hindi: मारूफगंज, IAST: mārūphagaṃja; Urdu: معروف گنج, romanized: maʿrūfganj), also spelled Marufganj, is a residential neighborhood under the jurisdiction of the Kotwali thana of Gaya, Bihar, India. In close proximity to the Gaya Junction railway station, it is one of the oldest and among the most crime-infested localities in the town.

items.note: Government Polytechnic, Gaya is a polytechnic institute in Bihar, India. It is administered by Department of Science and Technology, Bihar and is funded by Government of Bihar.

Forming the eastern wing of the larger Karimganj neighborhood, it shares its proximity with New Karimganj neighborhood to the west and Jagdeo Nagar to the north.

New Karimganj (Hindi: न्यू करीमगंज, IAST: Nyū Karīmagaṃja; Urdu: نیو کریم گنج, romanized: Niū Karīmganj), also spelled as New Kareemganj or simply Karimganj, is a residential neighborhood under the Civil Lines thana zone of Gaya, Bihar, India, inhabited mostly by Muslims. Forming the western wing of the larger Karimganj neighborhood, it shares its proximity with Old Karimganj neighborhood to the east and Jagdeo Nagar to the north.

Maroofganj (Hindi: मारूफगंज, IAST: mārūphagaṃja; Urdu: معروف گنج, romanized: maʿrūfganj), also spelled Marufganj, is a residential neighborhood under the jurisdiction of the Kotwali thana of Gaya, Bihar, India. In close proximity to the Gaya Junction railway station, it is one of the oldest and among the most crime-infested localities in the town.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Mangla Gauri Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Mangla Gauri Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Mangla Gauri Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Gaya International Airport (6 km) and the nearest railway station is Gaya Junction (2 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Mangla Gauri Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Mangla Gauri Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Mangla Gauri Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Mangla Gauri Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Mangla Gauri Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Government Polytechnic, Gaya, White House Compound (Gaya), Durga Bari (Gaya), Nazareth Academy, Gaya, Chatta Masjid.
